Title: Maize phenylalanine ammonia-lyases contribute to resistance to Sugarcane mosaic virus infection, most likely through positive regulation of salicylic acid accumulation

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling against pathogen infection, including antiviral resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of SA-responsive defense; transcript accumulation is reduced when ZmPALs are silenced and induced by SCMV infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPR1 transcript levels increased after SCMV infection. 2. ZmPR1 induction was compromised in ZmPAL-silenced plants. 3. The gene was used as an SA-response readout rather than a primary causal gene for metabolite production.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in systemically infected leaves after SCMV infection
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value in this paper; useful as defense marker for SA pathway activation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as SA-responsive marker gene, not a core mechanistic nutrient-metabolism gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling against pathogen infection, including antiviral resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Marker of SA-responsive defense; transcript accumulation is reduced when ZmPALs are silenced and induced by SCMV infection
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmPR5 transcript levels increased after SCMV infection. 2. ZmPR5 induction was reduced in ZmPAL-silenced plants. 3. The gene was used as an SA-response marker, not a core causal metabolism gene.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in systemically infected leaves after SCMV infection
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value in this paper; useful as defense marker for SA pathway activation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as SA-responsive marker gene, not a core mechanistic nutrient-metabolism gene.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: ZmPAL gene family (including ZmPAL, ZmPAL4, ZmPAL6, ZmPAL7, ZmPAL8, ZmPAL9; silenced collectively)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Phenylpropanoid pathway; salicylic acid biosynthesis via PAL-dependent route
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to phenylpropanoid metabolism feeding salicylic acid and lignin-associated phenolics
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream; first committed entry step into phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for salicylic acid accumulation and for downstream lignin/phenylpropanoid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported for SCMV-induced SA accumulation in maize
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antiviral defense signal limiting SCMV accumulation and symptom severity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of SA accumulation and resistance to SCMV; knockdown reduces SA and lignin accumulation and increases virus RNA/coat protein accumulation and symptom severity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SCMV infection significantly induced expression of multiple ZmPAL family members, especially ZmPAL, ZmPAL6, ZmPAL8 and ZmPAL9 by RT-qPCR, and ZmPAL/4/6/7/8/9 by RNA-seq. 2. BMV-VIGS knockdown of conserved ZmPAL sequences reduced expression of several family members and caused stronger stunting/necrosis, increased SCMV RNA and coat protein accumulation. 3. ZmPAL silencing decreased SA accumulation and reduced induction of SA-responsive genes ZmPR1 and ZmPR5. 4. Exogenous SA rescued resistance in ZmPAL-silenced plants, supporting that ZmPALs act upstream of SA-mediated antiviral defense. 5. ZmPALs also contributed to SCMV-induced lignin accumulation and broader phenylpropanoid metabolite increases.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in systemically infected leaves after SCMV infection, peaking around 9 dpi for tested members
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In maize B73, several family members are SCMV inducible; ZmPAL1 was not detected
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SCMV infection; exogenous SA pretreatment; BMV-mediated VIGS silencing
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; BMV-VIGS knockdown; SA metabolite quantification by UPLC-MS/MS; SCMV RNA/CP assays; lignin quantification; metabolomics; SA rescue assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: BMV-derived VIGS system for knockdown; no transgene promoter for metabolic engineering tested
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq at 9 dpi; metabolomics (accession not provided)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ZmPAL knockdown reduced lignin accumulation and increased virus susceptibility; no exogenous SA growth penalty detected at treatment concentration
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to enhance antiviral SA-mediated defense and phenylpropanoid/lignin metabolism in maize; likely useful for resistance breeding or engineering, but altered lignin/phenylpropanoid flux may have pleiotropic effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study functionally validates the ZmPAL family collectively rather than dissecting individual isoform-specific metabolic contributions.
